author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Arts Council Meet and Greet
summary: We are hosting a meet-and-greet event at the Spring Street Gallery this Saturday, December 13th, at 11:00 AM.
content:
We are hosting a meet-and-greet event at the Spring Street Gallery this Saturday, December 13th, at 11:00 AM.

We are looking to learn more about your experience as an artist on Block Island and how the Block Island Arts Council can help build a stronger creative community.
We are also looking for Art supporters who may have time or resources to share.

Please join us for some coffee, tea, snacks, and the opportunity to meet with some Block Island Arts Council Members!

All are welcome, and we hope to see you! Please note that there will be more future opportunities to connect and share with the BIAC if you can't make this event.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Santa at the Fire Barn
summary: The Block Island Volunteer Fire Department Woman’s Auxiliary is excited to invite all Island children—from birth through grade 4—to our cherished annual Christmas celebration with Santa and Mrs. Claus!
content:
The Block Island Volunteer Fire Department Woman’s Auxiliary is excited to invite all Island children—from birth through grade 4—to our cherished annual Christmas celebration with Santa and Mrs. Claus!

Santa and Mrs. Claus will have a special present for every child.
Before the big day, we’ll also be assembling our holiday baskets for our elderly residents on Thursday, December 11. To sweeten the season, we’re asking for help from our wonderful community bakers to provide 7 dozen cookies each, dropped off at the fire station by 4:00 PM on December 11.
These beloved traditions are made possible by your incredible generosity and support. If you’d like to contribute, we gratefully accept donations through our Venmo at @BIVFDAUX.
Thank you for helping us share the holiday spirit across our Island—it truly wouldn’t be the same without you.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island School Friends Pie Sale at The 1661 Inn
summary: The Block Island School Friends are excited to announce our Annual Thanksgiving Pie Sale—our biggest fundraiser of the year! This year’s sale will take place at our new location, The 1661 Inn.  We’ll have a delicious assortment of homemade pies and baked goods, and as always, all proceeds directly benefit student activities and programs at the Block Island School. Get your favorites early—these pies go fast!
content: The Block Island School Friends are excited to announce our Annual Thanksgiving Pie Sale—our biggest fundraiser of the year! This year’s sale will take place at our new location, The 1661 Inn.  We’ll have a delicious assortment of homemade pies and baked goods, and as always, all proceeds directly benefit student activities and programs at the Block Island School. Get your favorites early—these pies go fast!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Stuff the Cruiser Event at Block Island Grocery
summary: The NSPD is pleased to announce that our annual  "Stuff-A-Cruiser" event is scheduled for November 16th from 11am - 2pm in the Block Island Grocery Store parking lot.
content:
The NSPD is pleased to announce that our annual  "Stuff-A-Cruiser" event is scheduled for November 16th from 11am - 2pm in the Block Island Grocery Store parking lot.

We hope to fill a few of our NSPD Police SUVS  with food donations to support the island's Helping Hands food pantry!

 Please stop by, say hello and donate if you can!

Shelf stable food donations and grocery gift cards gratefully accepted!

(Please avoid donating expired foods, thx!)

Canned and jarred goods

-Canned vegetables 

  • Canned fruit 
  • Canned or pouched proteins (tuna, chicken, salmon, or beans) 
  • Soups and stews 
  • Spaghetti sauce 
  • Canned tomatoes 

Grains and pasta

  • Pasta and pasta sauce 
  • Rice (brown rice is a great option) 
  • Cereals 
  • Oatmeal (instant or rolled oats)
  • Pancake mix 

Other staples

  • Peanut butter and jelly  
  • Dried beans and lentils
  • Shelf-stable milk or milk substitutes 
  • Baking supplies (flour, sugar, baking soda, oil) 
  • Coffee, tea, and hot chocolate 
  • Snacks (granola bars, nuts, dried fruit) 
  • Also accepting baby formula   and diapers as well as personal hygiene products (soaps shampoo, toothpaste  )
  • * Please avoid donating expired foods as we cannot use them. Thanks!
address: Block Island New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: Block Island Maritime Institute Fish Release
summary: Help us send our fishy friends off on Sunday August 31st 11am - 1pm!
content:
Help us send our fishy friends off on Sunday August 31st 11am - 1pm!

 

These amazing species ambassadors have been helping us educate everyone on their importance within the ecosystem and the different ways we can help protect them and their home!

 

 Let’s give them a proper and enthusiastic send off together!

summary: When we look to nature we find a diverse spectrum of gender expressions and sexual behaviors, because nature is queer and being queer is natural.

title: 4th of July Parade
summary: Starts off at Legion Hall near The Oar.
content:
Starts off at Legion Hall near The Oar.

 

The route runs along West Side Road, starting at The Oar, following Ocean Avenue, Dodge Street, and Water Street ending at the roundabout at the statue of Rebecca. The parade theme is Block Island Lore & Legends.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Great Salt Pond Swim 10
summary: The Committee for the Great Salt Pond’s 10th annual swim, known as The Great Salt Pond Swim 10–2025.
content:
The Committee for the Great Salt Pond’s 10th annual swim, known as The Great Salt Pond Swim 10–2025.

This will be a one (1) mile swim limited to 150 individuals, starting at Andy’s Way Beach, swimming out toward Beane Point, and returning to Andy’s Way Beach.

The swimmer registration fee for the event is $60.00. Registration is now open. This is a great opportunity for first-time open water swimmers. The swim course is in a protected area with not much chop. In addition, the swim will take place along a straight line, marked by buoys.

This swim will be an annual fundraiser, serving to support our efforts to protect The Great Salt Pond, and provide funds for additional water testing in the recreational swim areas. 

Youth Great Salt Pond Swim

The Committee for the Great Salt Pond would like to announce our first youth swim, The

Great Salt Pond Youth Swim – 2024 on Saturday, July 26, 2025 at 9:30 am and it will be part of the Great Salt Pond Swim – 10 event. This will be a (1/4) quarter mile swim (not a race – no timing chips), ages 8–17, starting at Andy’s Way Beach, swimming out toward Beane Point, and returning to Andy’s Way Beach.

The swimmer registration fee for this event is $30.00, and on-line registration will open on January 1, 2025 and close on July 25, 2024, 1:00 pm. The link for on-line registration is to the right, and you can also go to Active.com to register. On-site registration will be available the morning of the swim, July 26th, until 8:15 am. The swim course is in a protected area with not much chop. In addition, the swim will take place along a straight line, marked by buoys. This swim event is our annual fundraiser, serving to support our efforts to protect The Great Salt Pond, and provide funds for additional water testing in the recreational swim areas.
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham Washington County

author: Austin Crawford
title: 41st Hal Madison Race
summary: The 41st edition of the Hal Madison Sailing Regatta will be held on Saturday, August 17th. Hosted by the Block Island Club, this event is open to all sailing yachts and dinghies. Please see the link below for more info and to register. We hope to see you for this wonderful and historic Block Island tradition!
content:
The 41st edition of the Hal Madison Sailing Regatta will be held on Saturday, August 17th. Hosted by the Block Island Club, this event is open to all sailing yachts and dinghies. Please see the link below for more info and to register. We hope to see you for this wonderful and historic Block Island tradition!

 

Saturday, August 17th, 0900: Skipper’s Meeting; Flagpole; Block Island Club, 136

Corn Neck Road.

Saturday, August 17th, 1100 First Gun with the intention of running one race with

a distance of 6-12 NM and an intended duration of approximately 3 hours.

Saturday, August 17th, 1600 Awards Ceremony.

Register: https://blockislandclub.org/regatta/lyHiPxgorn

author: Austin Crawford
title: Great Salt Pond Swim 9
summary: Swim registration will close July 26, 2024 at 1:00 pm.
content:
Swim registration will close July 26, 2024 at 1:00 pm.
There will be on-site registration in 2024.

The Committee for the Great Salt Pond’s eighth annual swim, known as The Great Salt Pond Swim 9–2024.

 

 

 

The Committee for the Great Salt Pond’s eighth annual swim, known as The Great Salt Pond Swim 9–2024.

This will be a one (1) mile swim limited to 150 individuals, starting at Andy’s Way Beach, swimming out toward Beane Point, and returning to Andy’s Way Beach.

The swimmer registration fee for the event is $60.00. Registration is now open. This is a great opportunity for first-time open water swimmers. The swim course is in a protected area with not much chop. In addition, the swim will take place along a straight line, marked by buoys.

This swim will be an annual fundraiser, serving to support our efforts to protect The Great Salt Pond, and provide funds for additional water testing in the recreational swim areas. 

New this year youth Great Salt Pond Swim

The Committee for the Great Salt Pond would like to announce our first youth swim, The Great Salt Pond Youth Swim – 2024 on Saturday, July 27, 2024 at 9:00 am and it will be part of the Great Salt Pond Swim – 9 event. This will be a (1/4) quarter mile swim (not a race – no timing chips), ages 8–17, starting at Andy’s Way Beach, swimming out toward Beane Point, and returning to Andy’s Way Beach.

The swimmer registration fee for this event is $30.00, and on-line registration will open on January 1, 2024 and close on July 26, 2024, 1:00 pm. The link for on-line registration is to the right, and you can also go to Active.com to register. On-site registration will be available the morning of the swim, July 27th, until 8:15 am. The swim course is in a protected area with not much chop. In addition, the swim will take place along a straight line, marked by buoys. This swim event is our annual fundraiser, serving to support our efforts to protect The Great Salt Pond, and provide funds for additional water testing in the recreational swim areas.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Great Salt Pond Swim 7
summary: Great Swimming Opportunity for First-time Open Water Swimmers
content:
Great Swimming Opportunity for First-time Open Water Swimmers

The Committee for the Great Salt Pond’s annual swim, The Great Salt Pond Swim 7-2022 will be held on Saturday, July 30, 2022 on Block Island, Rhode Island. This will be a (1) one mile swim limited to 150 individuals, starting at Andy’s Way Beach, swimming out toward Beane Point, and returning to Andy’s Way Beach. The swimmer registration fee for this event is $60.00, and on-line registration opens on October 1, 2021. This is a great opportunity for first-time open water swimmers and experienced swimmers. The swim course is in a protected area with not much chop. In addition, the swim will take place along a straight line, marked by buoys. This swim is an annual fundraiser, serving to support our efforts to protect The Great Salt Pond, and provide funds for additional water testing in the recreational swim areas. 

 

Register at https://www.active.com/block-island-ri/water-sports/swimming-races/great-salt-pond-swim-7-2022?int=72-3-A8
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham Washington County

summary: Steps off at The Oar.
content:

This year the parade theme will be "Celebrating Block Island." The route runs along West Side Road, starting at The Oar, following Ocean Avenue, Dodge Street, and Water Street ending at the roundabout at the statue of Rebecca.

content:
Please come and support the B.I. School Ceramics Department on Wednesday, May 25 from 5 to 7 p.m. You get to buy a hand-made bowl, sample a large variety of chilies and cornbread, vote for your favorite recipes and keep the bowl! All for a great cause. If you
have a chili recipe you would like to enter, please contact Lisa Robb at (401) 575-3167 for details. Fabulous prizes will be awarded for the best recipes!

author: Austin Crawford
title: Night Sky Viewing
summary: Lay back in the Hodge Preserve field while a local naturalist narrates the season’s constellations, planets, and maybe a shooting star. Dress warmly and bring a blanket/towel, beach chair, and flashlight for your viewing comfort. If the night sky is obscured, the program will be postponed to the following night. Sign up in advance to participate. Email: kim.gaffett@tnc.org.

author: Robert Lucier
title: July 4th Parade
summary: Don’t miss the annual Block Island July 4 parade, starting at 11 am from Legion Park.  Judging is at 9:30 am at the field in front of the Oar.
content:
Don’t miss the annual Block Island July 4 parade, starting at 11 am from Legion Park.  Judging is at 9:30 am at the field in front of the Oar.

Come early and take your place along the parade route so you can watch the floats, Uncle Sam, and bands playing all the way from Legion Park to Water Street.

For 2021, the parade theme is "Celebrate America."   The Grand Marshall will be the staff at the Block Island Post Office. Postmaster Becky Brown, and Tamzen A. Mazzur, Kim Bubko, and Kara Stinnett.
address: Water St New Shoreham Washington County

author: Robert Lucier
title: Winter Market at the Island Free Library
summary: The Island Free Library will not be holding the annual Winter Market this Sunday, Dec. 13. In keeping with local messaging and statewide precautions we are planning to host the Winter Market on Sunday, Dec. 13. This will be during the Governor’s proposed pause and we will be following all guidance and restrictions as outlined here.
content:
The Island Free Library will not be holding the annual Winter Market this Sunday, Dec. 13. In keeping with local messaging and statewide precautions we are planning to host the Winter Market on Sunday, Dec. 13. This will be during the Governor’s proposed pause and we will be following all guidance and restrictions as outlined here.

We plan to host vendors outside only and have already begun planning for a terrific day.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Block Island Historical Society Art Auction Fundraiser
summary: The BI Historical Society is hosting its second annual art and antique auction, this time under the tent in our very own backyard!
content:
The BI Historical Society is hosting its second annual art and antique auction, this time under the tent in our very own backyard!

Join us for champagne, wine, beer, refreshing Infused lemonade.,and appetizers plus a silent auction of art and antiques, this Saturday, July 13 5:00-8: 00 pm. Bidding will close st 7:30 pm.

$25 per person.  Helps fund future exhibits and preservation efforts.   Go home with valuable local art and antiques!

Preview Friday 11am- 4 pm. Reserve tickets by telephone or email.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: BI Historical Society Annual Meeting
summary: Please join us at The Narragansett Inn for The Annual Meeting and Program at 11 am.
content:
Please join us at The Narragansett Inn for The Annual Meeting and Program at 11 am.

The program is “Storm Stories”. There will be a panel of Islanders talking about their experiences of extreme weather here on the Island. Members are welcome.

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Block Island Music Festival
summary: If you’re into music – and we mean two stages, 6 days and 48 acts worth of music – there’s no place like The Block Island Music Festival. And the best part is – it’s free. Even the food. No kidding.
content:
If you’re into music – and we mean two stages, 6 days and 48 acts worth of music – there’s no place like The Block Island Music Festival. And the best part is – it’s free. Even the food. No kidding.

Now in its 19th year, The Block Island Music Festival brings the best up-and-coming original acts from all over the Northeast to Captain Nick's on Block Island starting June 19th.  

Running from Tuesday through Sunday, the festival features eight acts per day -- the first four acts taking place on the outdoor "Sunshine Stage" beginning at 5 pm (kids are welcome with strict parental supervision); the final four playing the Main Stage indoors, where the amps get cranked from 9 pm to 1 am (21+ only with proper ID required). 

The whole week has a special grassroots vibe.  The festival plays all types of music, creating a truly unique and exciting musical atmosphere. As the barbeque sizzles (amazingly, food is free until supplies run out for the day) and the blender churns out frozen drinks (not free, but really good), the audience slips easily into the mellow island atmosphere where flip flops and bikini tops constitute proper attire.

A word to the wise: you’ll want to stay over, and camping isn’t allowed on Block Island, so make hotel arrangements before you arrive – especially for the popular weekend dates.

 

 

Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

meta.title: Block Island Music Festival
meta.description: The 18th annual Block Island Music Festival from June 12 through June 2018.

address: New Shoreham New Shoreham Washington County

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: The Great Salt Pond Swim
summary: Great Swimming Opportunity for Both First-time and
content:
Great Swimming Opportunity for Both First-time and 

Experienced Open Water Swimmers

The Committee for the Great Salt Pond’s third annual swim, known as The Great Salt Pond Swim 2020.

Date: Saturday, July 25, 2020

Time: 11:00 am (On-Site Registration will close on July 25at 10:15 am)

Location: Andy's Way Beach • Block Island, Rhode Island

This will be a one (1) mile swim limited to 150 individuals, starting at Andy’s Way Beach, swimming out toward Beane Point, and returning to Andy’s Way Beach. 

The swimmer registration fee for the event is $55.00. Registration is now open. This is a great opportunity for first-time open water swimmers. The swim course is in a protected area with not much chop. In addition, the swim will take place along a straight line, marked by buoys.

This swim will be an annual fundraiser, serving to support our efforts to protect The Great Salt Pond, and provide funds for additional water testing in the recreational swim areas.  

Swim Participant Packet for more information, and be sure to visit our Facebook page.

Click here to view photos of the Great Salt Pond Swim 3–2017
address: Andy's Way New Shoreham New Shoreham

author: Austin Crawford
title: 2025 Block Island Half marathon
summary: Based on the current weather forecast of high winds and rain for Sunday and out of concern for the safety of all, We have decided to POSTPONE the Block Island half marathon.  The race will now be held on Sunday October 26th at 12:30 pm.
content:
Based on the current weather forecast of high winds and rain for Sunday and out of concern for the safety of all, We have decided to POSTPONE the Block Island half marathon.  The race will now be held on Sunday October 26th at 12:30 pm. 

This half marathon will begin and end at the Fred Benson Town Beach Pavilion. The beginning of the race heads north in a flat stretch followed by a gentle uplift to some steeper hills and turns. Runners will see the Great Salt Pond, beach dunes, The North Lighthouse and much more.

At the North Light,turn back and get ready for a challenging loop around the beautiful west side of the island. Lots of hills and turns. Rolling pastures, stone walls, farmhouses and the beautiful blue Atlantic are the backdrop.

The last stretch takes you down Airport Hill and back to the Pavillion.

October on Block Island is stunning!

 

Register for the event at https://www.active.com/block-island-ri/running/distance-running-races/block-island-half-marathon-2025
